per_turn_leaks.student_message: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: The pain is crampy and mostly on the left side of my abdomen, it feels like a sharp stabbing sensation that comes and goes. The blood in my stool is bright red and it's been happening with most bowel movements, which has also increased in frequency - I'm going to the bathroom way more often than usual.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, it's mostly confined to my abdomen, although I do get some aching in my knees and ankles from time to time - that's actually why I take ibuprofen for those symptoms. But when I'm having an episode with my abdominal pain and bloody stool, the discomfort is usually just focused on my lower belly.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I smoke occasionally, maybe one or two times a week when I'm stressed or celebrating something. And yeah, I do drink beer - usually 2-3 beers most weeknights after work to unwind. But lately, with this stress and all, I've been having more than that, probably 4-5 beers on some nights.
